rulings.Case Title: JESSIE MENDOZA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MAY 5, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT GENERAL STEAMSHIP AGENCIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled with 10 days to answer. The complaint is sufficient on the issue of successor liability. The objection to the social security records is sustained. =(302/PJM/VC) .

rulings.Case Title: JESSIE MENDOZA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MAY 5, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANTS CROWLEY MARITIME CORPORATION, MAERSK, INC., AMERICAN PRESIDENT LINES, LTD., MATSON NAVIGATION COMPANY, INC. AND NEDLLOYD LINES (USA) CORPORATION'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint: The motion to strike the punitive damages claim is granted. Punitive damages are not recoverable under the Jones Act. The 6th cause of action is for negligence and facts have not been alleged to state that claim or a basis for punitive damages. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend the 6th cause of action and may attempt to plead punitive damages as to that claim only. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JESSIE MENDOZA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, MAY 5, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANTS MAERSK, INC., AMERICAN PRESIDENT LINES, LTD., CROWLEY MARITIME CORPORATION, MATSON NAVIGATION COMPANY, INC. AND NEDLLOYD LINES (USA) CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT: Maersk et al. demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. This is the final opportunity for plaintiffs to amend. As to the 6th cause of action, paragraph 73 does not meet the test of Howlett v. Birkdale Shipping Co. S.A. (1994) 512 U.S. 92, in that it fails to address the standard of turning over the vessel to an expert stevedore. The allegations that these defendants had an "overarching responsibility" for certain areas is uncertain for the complaint lacks facts supporting any level of responsibility; nor is the Court aware of what is meant by "overarching responsibility". Plaintiffs allegations in paragraphs 77-79 regarding a "failure to monitor etc." and failure "to intervene etc." must be linked to facts reflecting the limited scope of defendants' liability under Howlett As to the 7th cause of action, plaintiffs' objections to defendants' evidence are sustained as not being appropriate for a demurrer. Plaintiffs state a cause of action by focusing in paragraph 89 on the vessel not being safe for the seaman's intended purpose, i.e. a safe place to live and work. As to the 8th and 9th causes of action, the demurrer is sustained. By incorporating the allegations of the 6th cause of action, plaintiffs unnecessarily create confusion and this is of consequence where there are different applicable legal standards that apply to longshoremen and seamen. As to the 10th cause of action, this is not a cause of action but a basis for plaintiffs standing to bring the action. Technically, defendants are correct and the demurrer is sustained. =(302/PJM/VC)
